response:
The error you encounter stems from a version mismatch between your .NET 7 application and the WPF assemblies it expects to load on Windows.

When targeting .NET 7 on macOS, the WPF project is excluded from compilation, but the Windows build still attempts to reference legacy .NET Framework 4.0 assemblies if the project configuration is not explicitly set to target .NET Core or .NET 5+.

cousin to this issue is the fact that WPF on .NET 5 and later requires the specific SDK and runtime packages to be installed on the development machine and the target deployment environment.

You must ensure your WPF project file explicitly targets net7.0-windows and that the PresentationFramework assembly is not being pulled in as a legacy dependency by adding a direct package reference or correcting the target framework declaration.

Finally, verify that the Windows machine has the .NET 7 desktop runtime installed and that your project does not contain any implicit references to System.Windows that default to the older framework version.
